idea.title: Shader Sell Job
idea.hook: Layer screen-space VFX on a plain prototype to hit a target 'feel score' before the publisher meeting — but each effect costs frame-budget you can't overspend.
idea.hook_ru: Накладывай скрин-спейс VFX на простой прототип, чтобы попасть в целевой 'feel score' до встречи с издателем — но каждый эффект жрёт бюджет кадра.
idea.prototype: Static gameplay clip loops in a window. Player has a palette of 8 VFX cards (chromatic aberration, bloom, dither, scanlines, vignette, parallax dust, screen shake, color grade). Each card has a feel-score gain and a frame-budget cost. Drag cards onto the clip; canvas re-composites live (cheap canvas filters/CSS-backdrop tricks). Daily target: hit feel >= X with frame-budget <= Y. Each level swaps the underlying clip (different genre) so the same palette behaves differently. Lose if you commit a build that misses target or busts budget.
idea.non_timer_pressure: Frame-budget cap + feel-score target (resource scarcity) + 3-strike lives. No countdown timer.
idea.lose_condition: Committed build either misses the feel-score target or exceeds the frame-budget cap (3-strike fail OR instant frame bust).
idea.why_novel: Distinct from 59_shader_polish_sprint (single-clip polish-budget) — this is per-level genre-clip swap with dual budget (feel + frame), strikes mechanic, and per-clip card-feel modifiers (same card behaves differently per genre).

sources.title: Production polish economics
sources.snippet: Indie dev polish phase is where most prototypes either snap into feel-right or die in over-stacked effects — the economics of which post-processes to ship are real.

sources.title: Post-process polish phase
sources.snippet: The polish phase is where indie devs stack shaders until the game 'feels right' — a tweetable craft skill nobody has gamified.

sources.title: Cheap screen-space VFX can sell a simple mechanic
sources.snippet: One shader-like effect can make a small prototype feel authored.

synthesizer_brief: Card-placement puzzler where 8 VFX cards (chromatic, bloom, dither, scanline, vignette, dust, shake, grade) play against TWO budgets (FEEL_TARGET + FRAME_BUDGET) on 6 swapping genre clips. Distinct from prior shader-polish game (single-clip budget). Live re-composite preview window + card palette + dual gauges. Mouse + touch drag, single COMMIT button per level. 3-strike lives.

alternatives.title: Ice Fracture Foreman
alternatives.hook: Tap a frozen lake to crack it in branching fissures, isolating fish into a shrinking pond before the cracks reach your hut
alternatives.why_novel: Corral/spatial puzzle with branching crack propagation

alternatives.title: Shader Saturday
alternatives.hook: Drag the post-process slider chain to match the day's screenshot — wrong order makes your render look like AI slop and judges roast you
alternatives.why_novel: Pixel-diff judged daily target vs Screenshot Saturday meme

alternatives.title: Token Throttle
alternatives.hook: You're a dispatcher routing tasks to cheap or expensive AI agents — pick wrong and your monthly budget burns before quota refills
alternatives.why_novel: Three-tier rework feedback loop on AI agent costs

alternatives.title: Tier-1 Pitch
alternatives.hook: Drag elements onto your store page — title, hook, icon, screenshot — and watch English-speaking testers either click Wishlist or bounce in disgust
alternatives.why_novel: Meta-game on Tim's own portfolio mistake about Tier-1 markets

alternatives.title: Tiny Markov Dunge
alternatives.hook: A 200-byte room-generator paints your dungeon in real time — your only weapon is editing the seed before the boss spawns from your own typo
alternatives.why_novel: Sub-1MB generator baked in; the game IS the data point
